attractions.name: Eco-Earth Globe
attractions.description: The Eco-Earth Globe is a massive 26-foot outdoor sculpture located in Salem's Riverfront Park along the Willamette River. Originally an industrial acid storage ball from a pulp and paper company, it was transformed into a community masterpiece covered in over 30,000 handmade ceramic tiles. The intricate mosaic, created by local artists and students, depicts the Earth's geography, diverse cultures, and various animal species.

attractions.description: The Willamette Valley Scenic Bikeway is the first official scenic bikeway in the United States, stretching 134 miles from Champoeg State Heritage Area to Eugene. Known for its friendly atmosphere and rich history, it is home to Western Oregon University and features a historic downtown with antique shops and galleries. The city serves as an ideal day trip destination for those looking to explore the scenic landscapes and wineries of the region.

highlights: ["Home to Western Oregon University, Oregon's oldest public university.", 'Historic Main Street featuring unique antique shops and local galleries.', 'Beautiful public parks including Main Street Park and Gentle Woods Park.', 'Outdoor amphitheater hosting community celebrations and musical performances.', 'Access to extensive bike trails and proximity to the Willamette River.', 'Located in the center of the Willamette Valley wine-growing region.']
attractions.tips: Take a relaxing stroll through the Western Oregon University campus to enjoy its historic architecture and lush landscaping. The town features a charming downtown with restored 19th-century buildings, over 30 public murals, and serves as the gateway to the renowned Silver Falls State Park and the 80-acre Oregon Garden. attractions.name: Gaiety Hill-Bush's Pasture Park Historic District (Travel Oregon)
attractions.description: The Gaiety Hill-Bush's Pasture Park Historic District is a 143-acre residential area featuring a concentration of homes and gardens dating from 1878 to 1938. It encompasses major landmarks such as the Bush House Museum, Deepwood Estate, and Gaiety Hollow, illustrating the evolutionary development of a neighborhood adjoining Salem's original town plat. The district is noted for its preservation of architectural styles and significant landscape architecture by the firm of Lord and Schryver (National Park Service, Travel Salem).

attractions.name: McCulloch Stadium (Charles McCulloch Stadium)
attractions.description: McCulloch Stadium is a historic 2,500-seat multi-use outdoor stadium located within Bush's Pasture Park in Salem, Oregon. Opened in 1950, it serves as the home venue for Willamette University's football, soccer, and track & field teams, as well as various high school athletic events. The facility features a covered grandstand, the Ted Ogdahl Field with FieldTurf, and the Charles Bowles Track. (Willamette University Athletics, Wikipedia)

attractions.description: Mount Angel Abbey is a historic Benedictine community of monks founded in 1882 on a hilltop overlooking Oregon’s Willamette Valley. The Abbey serves as a center for prayer, hospitality, and education, featuring a world-renowned library designed by Alvar Aalto and a Romanesque-style church where visitors can join the monks for daily prayer and chanting (Mount Angel Abbey).

attractions.name: Powerland Heritage Park Powerland Heritage Park
attractions.category: Heritage Museum / Open-Air Museum Powerland Heritage Park, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Powerland Heritage Park is a 62-acre community of 14 hands-on heritage museums dedicated to the preservation and operation of antique equipment and vehicles. Visitors can explore a vast collection of steam-powered machinery, vintage tractors, trolleys, and logging equipment that showcase the history of Oregon's agriculture and industry. The park hosts several major annual events, including the award-winning Great Oregon Steam-Up, where historic machines are brought to life for public demonstrations. attractions.name: The Gordon House
attractions.description: The Gordon House is the only design by Frank Lloyd Wright built in Oregon, originally designed in 1957 for Conrad and Evelyn Gordon. It is a prime example of Wright's Usonian architecture, emphasizing organic integration with nature and affordable, modern living. The house was deconstructed and moved from its original Wilsonville location to Silverton in 2001 to preserve it from demolition The Gordon House Conservancy, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 869 W Main Street, Silverton, Oregon 97381

attractions.name: Trail of Ten Falls at Silver Falls State Park
attractions.category: State Park / Hiking Trail (Oregon State Parks)
attractions.description: Known as the "crown jewel" of the Oregon State Parks system, Silver Falls State Park features the nationally recognized Trail of Ten Falls. This spectacular 7.2-mile loop weaves through a dense forested canyon, passing ten unique waterfalls, four of which hikers can walk behind. It offers breathtaking scenery, moderate hiking challenges, and historic facilities in a pristine natural setting (Oregon State Parks, Willamette Valley).
attractions.address: 20024 Silver Falls Hwy SE
attractions.hours: Nov-Jan: 8am-5pm; Feb: 8am-6pm; Mar: 8am-8pm; Apr-Aug: 7am-9pm; Sep: 7am-8pm; Oct: 8am-7pm (Oregon State Parks)

attractions.admission: $10 for Oregon residents, $12 for out-of-state residents (daily parking permit per vehicle); Free if walking, biking, or taking public transit (Oregon State Parks, Oregon State Parks News)
highlights: 'Hike a 7.2-mile loop visiting 10 distinct waterfalls ([Oregon State Parks)', 'Walk behind four massive waterfalls, including the iconic 177-foot South Falls (Wikipedia)', 'Explore a designated National Recreation Trail through lush Douglas fir forests (Oregon State Parks)', 'Visit the historic South Falls Lodge featuring a cafe and a cozy fireplace (Willamette Valley)', 'Enjoy year-round trail access with vibrant spring flows and colorful fall foliage (Willamette Valley)']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy, waterproof footwear as trails can be slippery and misty near the falls. Arrive early on weekends and during summer months to ensure parking availability.
